rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF RAYMOND J. MARTELLI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #8, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although reimbursements to any individual (in particular, the fiduciary) must clearly indicate who the original payee was (see reimbursements to the conservator of the estate on 4/6/23, 2/26/24, 3/4/24, 4/8/24, and 9/6/24). In light of Conservator's voluntarily reduced fee request, he will not be required to supplement these incompletely described reimbursements; however, future accountings must clearly identify the original payees. The date of the order granting fees to the former attorney for the conservatee was 4/5/24, not 4/30/24. The date of the order granting fees to the conservator and his attorney was 5/17/23, not 5/18/23. The bank statements filed on 4/25/25 were cut off, and at least one (Citi) was for the period ending 10/1/24, not 11/30/24.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALEXANDER F. BUIEL II
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Petition to clarify the nature of the additional $5,000 payable to the executor, determining whether it is an extraordinary "fee" or a conditional "gift" under the terms of the will. Even if all beneficiaries agree to the additional $5,000 as allowed in the will, it should not be recognized as compensation for services rendered, as the executor's statutory fees cannot exceed the statutory compensation limits despite beneficiaries' consents. Prob. Code sec. 10803. If the $5,000 must be characterized as "extraordinary fees," a necessary declaration must be submitted under CRC 7.703. Alternatively, if the additional $5,000 is to be considered a conditional gift with the beneficiaries' consent, the petition should outline this and make the appropriate request accordingly. A verified supplement clarifying this issue must be filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEAN YUKE CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Granted per written order, although the waivers of accounting and notice were of the original petition, not the amended petition, and there are apparently still securities on hand in the various IRA and investment accounts, but Administrator did not specifically set them forth, as required by CRC 7.651(b)(4). NB: If the assets distributed are not identical to the assets set forth in the Order for Final Distribution, the personal representative must file a fully noticed petition to seek approval of actions taken after the Order for Final Distribution was issued. [LRSF 14.35 I.6.d.]

Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Validity Of 2022 Amendment To Trust, For Financial Elder Abuse, And For Enhanced, Multiple, And Exemplary Damages Pusuant To Prob. Code 259, 850, 859
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Venue: the 16061.7 notice states that the successor trustee resides in San Mateo but the principal place of administration is San Francisco, his attorney's address. 2) Description of property the in the petition and Notice of Hearing is not specific as to the non-real property assets. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Determination Of Non-Liability Of Trust For Creditor'S Claim
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Note: No proposed order has been received from or lodged by petitioner. No proof of service of a copy of the proposed order required by LRSF 14.7(A)(4) has been filed by petitioner. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARIA VILMA MARTINEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/28/2025 to be heard with Melissa Chung's competing petition. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-121 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) If any heir is a citizen of El Salvador, notice must be given to the El Salvador Consulate. 4) Re item 8 of the petition: i) Petitioner's name, address, and relationship to the decedent must be listed. ii) Is petitioner a licensed real estate broker? iii) Sandra Rivas's address must be listed. See item 8 of Melissa Chung's competing petition on file.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HARRY JOSEPH BOBINEAUX
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/16/25 to be heard with the earlier petition filed in a different case. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An estate has already been opened for this decedent. This later case must be consolidated with PES-24-308029, or the judicial officer may ultimately just deny this later petition as unnecessary, if/when these two petitions are heard. (2) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (3) No proof of publication of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate has been filed. (4) No Receipt of Acknowledgment of the Duties and Liabilities has been filed. (5) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (6) If there are no assets (item 3 of the petition), why is an estate being opened? (7) Items 3e, 3f, 3g, and 3h of the petition are incomplete. (8) Items 5a(1) or (2), 5a(3) or (4), and 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. (9) Item 5b of the petition must be answered. (10) Neither the petition caption nor item 2c of the petition was completed to indicate that the petitioner seeks any IAEA authority. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any other papers to the assigned examiner by 6/2/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
